methods.description: Process for collecting pollen samples from apiary families and storing them for further analysis.

steps.description: 12 pollen samples collected from the same apiary families were investigated.
steps.searchable_summary: collect pollen samples from apiary families

steps.description: The collected fresh pollen samples (50 g) were stored at room temperature (20-23°C) in sterile paper bags for 1, 2 and 3 days.
steps.searchable_summary: store pollen samples at room temperature in sterile bags

methods.description: Determining the moisture content of fresh pollen samples by drying.

steps.description: Moisture content (%) of fresh pollen was determined by drying 5 g of samples in an oven at 105°C, until a constant weight.
steps.searchable_summary: dry pollen samples in oven to determine moisture content

methods.description: Isolation and culturing of fungi from pollen samples to determine fungal presence and concentration.

steps.description: Microscopic fungi were isolated by adding 10 g of ground pollen to 90 ml of physiological saline (NaCl, 8.5g/l) and shaking in a shaker for 15 min. (revolutions at 400 rpm).
steps.searchable_summary: isolate fungi by shaking pollen in saline solution

steps.description: A series of dilutions were prepared from the resulting suspension.
1 ml of 10^-2, 10^-3 and 10^-4 suspensions were dispensed in Petri dishes (8 cm diameter) on which the Potato Dextrose Agar (PDA) medium was infused.
steps.searchable_summary: dispense diluted suspensions on PDA medium

steps.description: For extracting the yeast, 0.1 ml of 10^-1, 10^-2 and 10^-3 suspensions were spread in Petri dishes over a suspended Saboraud Dextrose Agar (SDA) medium and dispersed with a Drigalski lens.
steps.searchable_summary: spread yeast suspensions on SDA medium

steps.description: Petri dishes with cultures for 5-7 days were maintained in a thermostat at 26 ± 2°C, and for the isolation of yeast the plates with cultures were maintained for 2-4 days, at 22°C.
Grown fungi were counted and evaluated as colony-forming units per gram of bee pollen (CFU/g).
steps.searchable_summary: count and evaluate fungi as CFU per gram

steps.description: Morphological features of the colonies of the raised fungi were evaluated macroscopically (colony occurrence) and examined by light microscopy.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate fungal colonies macroscopically and microscopically

steps.description: Separate fungal colonies were purified to monocultures and identified by cultural and morphological features based on various descriptors.

methods.description: Analysis of mycotoxins in pollen samples using ELISA method.

steps.description: The mycotoxins deoxynivalenol (DON), zearalenone (ZEN) and T-2 toxin (T-2) were analysed by ELISA (enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay) method.
The RIDASCREEN® quantitative test kits were used for the analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: use RIDASCREEN test kits for mycotoxin analysis

steps.description: The optical densities of samples and controls from standard curve were estimated by a photometer Neogen Stat Fax®303 Plus, using filter of 450 nm.
Measured absorbances were converted to the mycotoxin concentration units -μg × kg -1.

methods.description: Statistical analysis of experimental data to evaluate significance of differences between treatments.

steps.description: The significance of the differences between the treatments was evaluated applying F criteria and the least significant difference (LSD) test.
steps.searchable_summary: apply F criteria and LSD test for significance evaluation

steps.description: The statistical analysis of the experimental data was performed using the software ANOVA from the package SELEKCIJA.
steps.searchable_summary: perform statistical analysis using ANOVA software

steps.description: Confidence limits were added at P < 0.05.
